txt_nvda10K2025.md_item_1_3.summary: NVIDIA has invested heavily in research and development, totaling over $76.7 billion since its inception, leading to significant innovations like GPUs, CUDA, and AI technologies. Key milestones include the invention of the GPU in 1999, the introduction of CUDA in 2006, and the acquisition of Mellanox in 2020, which expanded their product offerings into networking and data center solutions. NVIDIA has also developed comprehensive software stacks such as DRIVE for autonomous driving, Clara for healthcare, Omniverse for physical AI applications, and NVIDIA AI Enterprise, establishing itself as a leader in enterprise AI.

txt_nvda10K2025.md_item_1_6.summary: NVIDIA, headquartered in Santa Clara, California, was incorporated in 1993 and reincorporated in Delaware in 1998. NVIDIA addresses four key markets—Data Center, Gaming, Professional Visualization, and Automotive—through its advanced computing platforms.
